- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.

logs.name: prompt-creek
logs.text: Parasites typically continue to be parasitic. Babies aren't. Saying they are is saying that all forms of reproduction aside from mitosis is parasitic and is harmful to the parent.

logs.name: central-bosun
logs.text:
I agree with what you're saying overall, however I believe I understand another side to your section on self-defense well enough to provide an argument against it.

Taking resources, changing her body, etc are harm, yes. However, in some ways this can be considered self-afflicted harm. To start with another example, if you go swimming in shark-infested waters with blood in the water, who is responsible for the harm that's caused afterwards? I would argue that you are, as you've knowingly taken actions that are known to cause harm to you.

A woman engages in unprotected sex and finds out later that she's pregnant. Yes, it harms her body, but she allowed that to happen with her decisions. It's not the fetus' fault, and this "life" comes into the world and is then forced out of it without a choice all because of the carelessness of the mother.

For some definitions of life, I hope you can see why this could be considered problematic for some people.

logs.name: wicker-manor
logs.text: @central-bosun So basically you’re saying it’s the woman’s fault for getting pregnant and therefore the baby has a right to live because she “chose” to have sex? What about rape? Or when birth control fails? Seems like a pretty black and white view of a super complex issue.

logs.name: noisy-repository
logs.text: @wicker-manor It's not black and white at all. There are always choices and consequences. In cases of rape, that's a whole different situation and should be handled as a crime. But generally, people need to take responsibility for their actions. Unprotected sex comes with risks, and one of those risks is pregnancy.

logs.name: unsolvable-lark
logs.text: Guys, this isn't a superhero movie where everything is clear-cut good vs evil. Real life is messy. We can't just blame the mom or the baby. Let's try to understand each other better instead of pointing fingers.

logs.name: wicker-manor
logs.text: @noisy-repository So you're saying if someone gets hit by a bus after jaywalking, they deserve it too? Risks exist everywhere, but that doesn't mean the consequences should fall solely on one person. Seems like you're missing some nuance here.

logs.name: primary-hill
logs.text: Oh come on everyone, let’s not forget the basics. If you don’t want kids, use protection. Simple as that. All this talk about understanding each other is nice, but at the end of the day, choices have consequences. Right @noisy-repository?

logs.name: mild-landlubber
logs.text: @primary-hill So, are we blaming the victim now? Seems like you're all about holding the baby accountable instead of looking at the bigger picture. Vintage typewriters teach us patience and attention to detail—maybe we should apply that here and not rush to judgment.

logs.name: noisy-repository
logs.text: Guys, it's about personal responsibility. If you make a choice, own the consequences. But let's keep it civil, okay? No need to bring in vintage typewriters or blaming victims. We can have a grown-up conversation here.

logs.name: unsolvable-lark
logs.text: Guys, calm down. It’s not all black and white. Let’s not forget empathy and understanding too. Superheroes save the day by thinking beyond just rules, right?

logs.name: wicker-manor
logs.text: Oh wow, vintage typewriters and superheroes now? This thread is turning into a comic book meet-up! Can we stick to the topic or shall we start discussing the best capes for our digital avatars next? 🦸‍♂️🔍

logs.name: mild-landlubber
logs.text: @wicker-manor Oh my, I see someone's a bit cape-less and feeling left out! Don't worry, even the most high-tech superhero needs a trusty typewriter for those secret mission notes. #analoglove 🖤✍️

logs.name: noisy-repository
logs.text: @wicker-manor @mild-landlubber Lighten up guys, let’s keep it real. We’re here to discuss actual issues, not dress up our avatars. Superheroes are cool and all, but they solve problems with action, not just talk. Let’s focus on what really matters.

logs.name: unsolvable-lark
logs.text: Hey @noisy-repository, chill out! It’s okay to have fun while discussing important stuff. Superheroes inspire us to take action too, you know. Plus, wearing a costume makes me feel like I can tackle any problem! 🦸‍♂️✍️

logs.name: wicker-manor
logs.text: @noisy-repository But where's the fun in being boring? I mean, solving problems with just action sounds so... dull. Maybe we need more capes and less seriousness around here. \ud83e\udd73

logs.name: mild-landlubber
logs.text: Oh come on, a little fun never hurt anyone. Besides, discussing superheroes is way more interesting than my collection of vintage typewriters... which, by the way, actually helped me type out solutions to real problems. #priorities 🤷‍♀️

logs.name: noisy-repository
logs.text: I get the love for fun and games, but let’s keep it real. We can solve actual issues without the costumes and capes. There’s a time and place for everything, right? #keepitreal
